heading: Definitions
label: 17191 Definitions
notes: Amendments2020—Par. (1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) defined the term “engineered”.Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(a)Pars. (2) to (8). , (3), added par. (2) and redesignated former pars. (2) to (7) as (3) to (8), respectively.Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(a)(2)
Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2
Short TitleThis part known as the “Advanced Geothermal Energy Research and Development Act of 2007”, see Short Title note set out under .section 17001 of this title
amendments: Amendments2020—Par. (1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) defined the term “engineered”.Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(a)Pars. (2) to (8). , (3), added par. (2) and redesignated former pars. (2) to (7) as (3) to (8), respectively.Pub. L. 116–260, § 3002(a)(2)

children.label: A
children.text: Lack of effective permeability, porosity or open fracture connectivity within the heat reservoir.

children.label: B
children.text: Insufficient contained geofluid in the heat reservoir.

children.label: C
children.text: A low average geothermal gradient which necessitates deeper drilling, or the use of alternative heat sources or heat generation processes.

children.label: G
children.text: A consortium of 2 or more entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (F).

children.heading: Enhanced geothermal systems
children.label: 3 Enhanced geothermal systems
children.text: The term “enhanced geothermal systems” means geothermal reservoir systems that are engineered, as opposed to occurring naturally.

children.heading: Geofluid
children.label: 4 Geofluid
children.text: The term “geofluid” means any fluid used to extract thermal energy from the Earth which is transported to the surface for direct use or electric power generation, except that such term shall not include oil or natural gas.

children.heading: Geopressured resources
children.label: 5 Geopressured resources
children.text: The term “geopressured resources” mean geothermal deposits found in sedimentary rocks under higher than normal pressure and saturated with gas or methane.

children.heading: Geothermal
children.label: 6 Geothermal
children.text: The term “geothermal” refers to heat energy stored in the Earth’s crust that can be accessed for direct use or electric power generation.

children.heading: Hydrothermal
children.label: 7 Hydrothermal
children.text: The term “hydrothermal” refers to naturally occurring subsurface reservoirs of hot water or steam.

children.heading: Systems approach
children.label: 8 Systems approach
children.text: The term “systems approach” means an approach to solving problems or designing systems that attempts to optimize the performance of the overall system, rather than a particular component of the system.
